chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step guide on how to scale the custom density parameter for an offset in a Rheonics SRD sensor, including preparation, calibration, and software baseline verification.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
How to scale the custom density parameter for an offset in the SRD sensor?
Modified on: Wed, 30 Nov, 2022 at 3:21 PM

Which Products are Involved?
SRD (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-density-meter-srd/)
DVM (https://rheonics.com/products/hpht-pvt-density-meter-viscometer-dvm/)
DVP (https://rheonics.com/products/inline-gas-lng-density-meter-dvp/)
SME-DRM (https://rheonics.com/electronics-and-communication/)
RCP
For SME-DRM (DIN rail mount) transmitter. Visit our product page to learn more about the full features.
Visit Rheonics product page: https://rheonics.com/products/ (https://rheonics.com/products/)

1. Preparation before scaling
If the sensor is to be installed in a tank, the sensor must be fully in the fluid. We must ensure that the sensing element is fully wetted and that the sensing area is unobstructed. Then, the sensor will measure the viscosity and/or density of the wetted area of the sensing element.
Figure 1. Sensor installed in a water tank

2. When one-point calibration is necessary to get accurate measurements in applications where the SRD is installed in a tank with water, the RCP software is required.

3. Verify that baseline is correct for the sensor with the RCP software, in our scenario an SRD is used so
Figure 2. Baseline verification

4. Go to the “Scaling” tab (This requires enabling expert mode), there open the “Select Measurement”
key_facts: The sensor must be fully submerged in the fluid if installed in a tank.
The sensing element must be fully wetted and its area unobstructed for accurate measurements.
The sensor measures the viscosity and/or density of the wetted area of the sensing element.
RCP software is required for accurate one-point calibration of the SRD sensor, especially when it's installed in a water tank.
Verifying the baseline is correct for the sensor using the RCP software is a necessary step.
Accessing the "Scaling" tab in the RCP software requires enabling expert mode.
diagram_labels: Sensing element must be always in fluid
warnings_notes: The sensor's sensing element must be fully wetted and its sensing area unobstructed to ensure proper measurement.
Enabling 'expert mode' is required to access the "Scaling" tab in the RCP software.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a guide on performing in-field calibration correction using the Rheonics Control Panel software, detailing steps for density value selection, scaling methods, and sensor readings.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.2

Tabs: Measurement, Events, Settings, Service, Communication, Scaling, Calculation, Logger, Help

Window Title: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

Main Controls:
  Refresh button
  Load Sensor State button

Sensor Status Panel:
  Air Check: Air (active in Fig 3 & 4), Liquid (active in Fig 5)
  Sensor Clean: Clean button

Scaling Menu Panel:
  Save Scaling Record button
  Load Scaling Profile button
  Scaling History button
  Help button
  Field Calib. with STCM button
  Tutorial button

Scale Output Controls:
  Select Measurement dropdown (currently 'Calc. Dens.' selected, options include Viscosity, Density, Temperature, Kinematic Viscosity, Calc. Visc., Concentration)
  Unscaled/Uncalibrated value: 0 g/cc (Fig 3 & 4), 996.178 kg/m3 (Fig 5)
  Scaled/Calibrated value: 0 g/cc (Fig 3), 1 g/cc (Fig 4)
  Scaling Methods: Single Point Scaling button, Two Point Scaling button
  Sensor Read Out: Read button, value: 0 g/cc (Fig 3, 4, 5)
  Expected Value: Enter -> value: 0 g/cc (Fig 3 & 5), 1 g/cc (Fig 4), Write button

Scaling Coefficients Table:
  Read Scaling Coefficients button
  Coefficients:
    C0: Current 0.000000, User (estimated) 0.000000, Factory 0.000000
    C1: Current 1.000000, User (estimated) 1.000000, Factory 1.000000
  SCALE button
  Switch (User / Factory buttons)

Figure Captions:
  Figure 3. Selecting the custom density value.
  Figure 4. Unscaled and scaled values from the RCP
  Figure 5. SRD starts readings the fluid density. Density unit kg/m3

Instructions:
  5. When a parameter is selected, the software is going to automatically populate the unscaled value and the scaled value
  6. Adding liquid(Water) to the tank. The readings will be affected by this change in test conditions.
  7. Select the scaling method, “Single Point” is to be used for offset calibration (should be used when eith
  8. After selecting the “Single Point Scaling”, click on the read button, this will populate the “Sensor Read Out” box with the raw value currently being measured by the sensor.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) software version shown is 5.4.0.2.
The software automatically populates unscaled and scaled values when a measurement parameter is selected.
Adding liquid (e.g., Water) to the tank will affect sensor readings.
The 'Single Point Scaling' method is designated for offset calibration.
Clicking the 'Read' button after selecting 'Single Point Scaling' will display the raw sensor measurement in the 'Sensor Read Out' box.
The software can display fluid density in units of g/cc and kg/m3.
The scaling coefficients (C0 and C1) have 'Current', 'User (estimated)', and 'Factory' values.
The Sensor Status panel includes checks for 'Air' and 'Liquid' conditions, and a 'Sensor Clean' option.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a step-by-step guide on how to perform in-field calibration correction using the Rheonics Control Panel software, specifically for density measurements.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.2
Measurement Events Settings Service Communication Scaling Calculation Logger
Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction
Select Measurement: Calc. Dens.
Unscaled/Uncalibrated: 993.271 g/cc
Scaled/Calibrated: 993.271 g/cc
Single Point Scaling
Two Point Scaling
Sensor Read Out: Read, 993.776 g/cc, Set, Read 1, g/cc, Set
Expected Value: Enter -> 0 g/cc, Write, Enter -> 1 g/cc, Write
Scaling Coefficients: Read Scaling Coefficients
Current: C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
User (estimated): C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
Factory: C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
Figure 6. Click-in Sensor Read Out and populate

9. Save the read parameter (Sample 1) by click-in the button "set"

Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.2
Measurement Events Settings Service Communication Scaling Calculation Logger
Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction
Select Measurement: Calc. Dens.
Unscaled/Uncalibrated: 993.064 g/cc
Scaled/Calibrated: 993.064 g/cc
Single Point Scaling
Two Point Scaling
Sensor Read Out: Read, 993.776 g/cc, Set, Read 1 993.776 g/cc, Set
Expected Value: Enter -> 0 g/cc, Write, Enter -> 1 g/cc, Write
Scaling Coefficients: Read Scaling Coefficients
Current: C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
User (estimated): C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
Factory: C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
Figure 7. Saving Sample 1 by click-in set button

10. Write the “expected value” for the measurement, for the offset calibration this means that the sensor output is higher or lower than the expected reference value. Click “Write” to store the value in SMET.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.2
Measurement Events Settings Service Communication Scaling Calculation Logger
Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction
Select Measurement: Calc. Dens.
Unscaled/Uncalibrated: 993.026 g/cc
Scaled/Calibrated: 993.026 g/cc
Single Point Scaling
Two Point Scaling
Sensor Read Out: Read, 993.776 g/cc, Set, Read 1 993.776 g/cc, Set
Expected Value: Enter -> 1100 g/cc, Write, Enter -> 1 g/cc, Write
Scaling Coefficients: Read Scaling Coefficients
Current: C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
User (estimated): C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
Factory: C0 0.000000, C1 1.000000
Figure 8. Saving lab value 1 of the expected value.

11. Click “SCALE” to use the stored samples to scale the SMET output. In this example notice that the
key_facts: The document outlines a multi-step procedure for in-field calibration correction using the Rheonics Control Panel software version 5.4.0.2.
Calibration involves reading sensor data, saving it as a sample, and entering a corresponding expected reference value.
Offset calibration requires comparing the sensor output to an expected reference value.
The 'Set' button is used to save read parameters (samples), and the 'Write' button stores expected values in SMET.
The process aims to scale the SMET output using stored samples.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software is used for in-field calibration correction, specifically for calculated density.
The SCALE BUTTON is used to load stored samples for calibration.
During the calibration process, the closest value is used to fix the expected value.
Scaling coefficients (C0 and C1) can be read from 'Current', 'User (estimated)', and 'Factory' categories.
After scaling, new unscaled/uncalibrated and scaled/calibrated values become observable in their respective display boxes.
The displayed examples demonstrate a change in the C0 coefficient from 0.000000 to 106.223877 after reading scaled coefficients.
diagram_labels: Scale Output - Lab Correlations - In-field Calibration Correction
Figure 9. SCALE BUTTON to load the stored samples, we noticed the closest value to fix the expected value.
